commands.review_summary:
The branch makes shared-chat auto-reply prompt wording use channel/thread language when `ChatType` is `channel`, preserves group wording for group chats, and adds regression coverage plus a prompt snapshot refresh.

PR surface: Source +26, Tests +24. Total +50 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main maps Mattermost public channels to `ChatType: "channel"`, routes that through `buildGroupChatContext`, and the helper emits group-chat wording. I did not run a live Mattermost repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The actionable blocker is contributor real behavior proof plus normal CI/review, not a narrow code repair task.

target.title: fix(auto-reply): align channel intro wording with chat_type
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
Mattermost channel turns are currently described in the agent prompt as group chats, and their delivery guidance refers to group destinations. That wording is inaccurate for channel conversations and can steer replies toward the wrong destination semantics.

## Why This Change Was Made
Derive the shared-chat wording from the authoritative normalized `ChatType`: channels use channel/thread wording, while groups retain group-chat wording. The tool-only silence guidance follows the same destination label. Regression coverage covers Mattermost channels and the existing WhatsApp group wording.

## User Impact
Mattermost channel prompts now identify the conversation as a channel and consistently describe automatic and message-tool-only delivery. Group-chat prompts keep their existing behavior.

## Evidence
- `vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply.triggers.trigger-handling.targets-active-session-native-stop.e2e.test.ts` (21 passed)
- `vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts` (7 passed)
- `oxlint --deny-warnings` on touched auto-reply files
- `oxfmt --check` and `git diff --check`
- Fresh autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings

Closes #95645
